headline: Why Iran has yet to confirm its presence in Pakistan for a new round of negotiations with the US
subhead: Despite differences between diplomats and military officials, Iranian authorities have largely presented a united front toward the United States. They have refused to negotiate under pressure and have demanded prior guarantees, especially regarding the lifting of the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: Neither war nor peace: Trump unilaterally extends ceasefire with Iran

The Belgian PM's troubling ambivalence toward Russia
subhead: Bart De Wever faces criticism for his unclear stances on the level of support the European Union should provide to Ukraine. The Flemish nationalist has defended himself against accusations of playing into Moscow's hands, insisting that he only aims to push the 27 member states forward.

headline: World Cup fans face soaring prices as transport costs skyrocket in the US
subhead: Massachusetts and New Jersey transit authorities have announced dramatic fare hikes for round-trip train journeys to stadiums. The move has drawn sharp criticism from fans, already grappling with steep costs to attend the tournament.

headline: 'Why am I alive?': The Yazidi genocide as told by one of its survivors
subhead: Samih Taha, now a refugee in France, is one of the few survivors of the Islamic State group massacre of his village in northern Iraq on August 15, 2014. Then 17 years old, he went into debt to buy back his mother and sisters, who had been forced into sexual slavery. He told Le Monde his story and the tragedy that befell his family.

headline: France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help: 'The mineralized bones literally are exploding'
subhead: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.

subhead: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

headline: France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
subhead: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.

headline: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
subhead: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.
